stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Most, but not all, of the carbon stored in the deep ocean comes from sinking organic matter from surface waters.
  • Oceanographer Lena Brandt from Stockholm University wondered if some of the unaccounted-for deep-ocean carbon could be coming from underwater volcanic vents.
  • In 2017, she analyzed data from the deep-sea probe Nereid.
  • Nereid measured dissolved gases and minerals it encountered while drifting near vents on the Mid-Atlantic Ridge.
  • Based on the Nereid data, Brandt confirmed her suspicion that underwater vents contribute substantial carbon to the deep ocean.
stem: The student wants to emphasize the aim of the research study. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Oceanographer Lena Brandt set out to determine whether some of the deep ocean’s carbon was coming from underwater volcanic vents.
Lena Brandt’s study determined that underwater vents contribute a substantial amount of carbon to the deep ocean.
As it drifted along the Mid-Atlantic Ridge, the Nereid probe collected data that was later analyzed by oceanographer Lena Brandt.
Before 2017, Lena Brandt wondered if the deep ocean was receiving some of its carbon from underwater vents.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The sentence emphasizes the aim, or goal, of the research study, noting what Brandt set out to do: determine whether some of the deep ocean’s carbon was coming from underwater volcanic vents.

Choice B is incorrect. The sentence presents the study’s conclusion; it doesn’t emphasize the aim. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence focuses on how the Nereid probe collected data; it doesn’t emphasize the aim of the research study. Choice D is incorrect. While the sentence mentions what Brandt was curious about before the study, it doesn’t emphasize her study’s aim.
